articles.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### MCP is dead; long live MCP
URL: https://chrlschn.dev/blog/2026/03/mcp-is-dead-long-live-mcp/
Model Context Protocol hits inflection point — architectural shift incoming
Anthropic's MCP standard for tool-calling is getting a significant overhaul, suggesting the first-gen protocol hit scaling walls faster than expected. Watch for downstream effects on the agentic tooling ecosystem built on current spec.

### Lies I was Told About Collaborative Editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs
URL: https://www.moment.dev/blog/lies-i-was-told-pt-2
CRDT orthodoxy challenged — Yjs may not be the default answer for real-time collaboration
Moment.dev's engineering team details why the dominant collaborative editing library failed their use case. Relevant for any due diligence on multiplayer SaaS infrastructure plays — the "just use Yjs" conventional wisdom has meaningful caveats.

### SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads
URL: https://atgreen.github.io/repl-yell/posts/sbcl-fibers/
Common Lisp gets modern concurrency primitives — niche but signals broader runtime innovation
Steel Bank Common Lisp adding fiber support follows Go and Java's virtual threads playbook. Indicates continued investment in alternative runtime ecosystems outside the Python/JS monoculture.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/us-army-announces-contract-with-anduril-worth-up-to-20b/
Anduril cements position as DoD's software-native prime contractor
$20B ceiling consolidating 120+ procurement actions into single enterprise contract. This is the Pentagon acknowledging traditional primes can't deliver integrated software platforms. Validates the Palantir/Anduril thesis and creates massive moat for subsequent programs.

### Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/meta-reportedly-considering-layoffs-that-could-affect-20-of-the-company/
Meta potentially cutting 15-20K heads to fund AI infrastructure buildout
The "Year of Efficiency" becomes permanent operating posture. 20% headcount reduction funds capex without margin compression. Watch for talent diaspora into AI startups and competitive hiring dynamics at OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind.

### Honda is killing its EVs — and any chance of competing in the future
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/honda-is-killing-its-evs-and-any-chance-of-competing-in-the-future/
Honda exits US EV market — legacy OEM capitulation accelerates
Japanese automaker abandoning three US EV models signals broader retreat from electrification timeline. Creates opportunity for Chinese OEMs if tariff regime shifts, and validates Tesla/BYD structural advantages. Honda market cap implications worth monitoring.

### 'Not built right the first time' — Musk's xAI is starting over again, again
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/not-built-right-the-first-time-musks-xai-is-starting-over-again-again/
xAI reboots coding tool effort, poaches Cursor executives
Third restart on coding assistant suggests fundamental execution issues despite $100B+ compute buildout. Cursor talent acquisition signals market validation for that product category while highlighting xAI's build-vs-buy desperation.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/lawyer-behind-ai-psychosis-cases-warns-of-mass-casualty-risks/
AI safety litigation moves from suicide cases to mass casualty events
Plaintiffs' attorney signals expansion of AI harm cases beyond individual incidents. Regulatory and liability frameworks remain undefined while deployment accelerates. Insurance and indemnification structures for AI products become material diligence items.

### Companies House vulnerability enabled company hijacking
URL: https://taxpolicy.org.uk/2026/03/13/companies-house-security-vulnerability-directors-addresses/
UK corporate registry security flaw allowed hostile takeovers of legitimate companies
Government identity infrastructure vulnerabilities create systemic risk for any business relying on official records for KYC/verification. Relevant for fintech, regtech, and any platform trusting government data sources as authoritative.

### The MacBook Neo is 'the most repairable MacBook' in years, according to iFixit
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/the-macbook-neo-is-the-most-repairable-macbook-in-years-according-to-ifixit/
Apple reverses 14-year repairability decline — regulatory pressure works
Right-to-repair legislation finally forcing design changes at Cupertino. iFixit validation matters for enterprise procurement and sustainability mandates. Watch for similar shifts across consumer electronics as EU regulations bite.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

Defense tech is the new enterprise SaaS — Anduril's $20B contract proves the Pentagon has decided software-native contractors are the future, not a supplement to legacy primes. Meanwhile, the AI infrastructure race is cannibalizing headcount across Big Tech (Meta's 20% cut, xAI's executive poaching), suggesting we're entering a phase where compute capex crowds out human capital. The litigation angle on AI harms is underappreciated — when plaintiffs' lawyers start connecting chatbots to mass casualty events, the liability overhang on consumer AI deployments becomes material to valuation.
